title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant threat to organizations relying on cloud services, as it exploits compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code. This could lead to data encryption, service outages, financial losses, and reputational damage.
recommended_actions: Implement strict access controls and monitor CI/CD pipelines for unauthorized changes or suspicious activity.
Regularly back up critical data and ensure backups are stored in isolated environments to prevent ransomware from encrypting them.
Deploy endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions and conduct regular security audits of cloud configurations.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: A security flaw has been discovered in AstrBotDevs AstrBot 4.23.6. This vulnerability affects...
description: A security flaw has been discovered in AstrBotDevs AstrBot 4.23.6. This vulnerability affects unknown code of the file /api/skills/delete of the component API Endpoint. Performing a manipulation of the argument Name results in path traversal. The attack can be initiated remotely. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in AstrBot 4.23.6 allows remote attackers to perform path traversal via the 'Name' parameter in the /api/skills/delete API endpoint. This could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive files and data, potentially compromising system integrity and confidentiality.
recommended_actions: Immediately implement input validation and sanitization for the 'Name' parameter to prevent path traversal attacks.
Update or patch the affected AstrBot software if a fix becomes available from the vendor.
Monitor systems for unusual activity and consider deploying a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and block exploitation attempts.

description: A security vulnerability has been detected in Dolibarr ERP CRM up to 23.0.1. The attack may be initiated remotely. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used. Upgrading to version 23.0.2 is recommended to address this issue. The identifier of the patch is ee93b6f2f9dd0f6aeefe9d718ab3ab0a443

This patch is called 16d9b449c9aa53ccee44144a762a2737d7ba4fc4. It is

The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

The exploit is publicly available, increasing the likelihood of exploitation.
recommended_actions: Immediately restrict access to the vulnerable endpoint (develop/systparam/softlogo/upload.jsp) using web application firewalls or access control lists.
Implement input validation and file type restrictions on the affected upload functionality to prevent malicious file uploads.
Contact the vendor for updates or patches, and consider migrating to an alternative CRM solution if no response or fix is provided.

title: A vulnerability has been found in Assimp up to 6.0.4. Affected by this issue is the function...
description: A vulnerability has been found in Assimp up to 6.0.4. Affected by this issue is the function glTF2::LazyDict in the library glTF2Asset.h. Such manipulation of the argument operator[] leads to null pointer dereference. The attack must be carried out locally. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used. The name of the patch is d24b85319bd70c65883a2b96613e07e23fb95981. It is best practice to apply a patch to resolve this issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Assimp up to version 6.0.4 could lead to a null pointer dereference, potentially causing application crashes or denial of service. However, the exploit requires local access, reducing its overall severity.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply the patch identified as d24b85319bd70c65883a2b96613e07e23fb95981 to mitigate the vulnerability.
Review and update all systems and applications using Assimp to ensure they are running a patched version.
Conduct a code review to identify any additional vulnerabilities or improper handling of null pointers in related libraries.

This could potentially cause application crashes or denial of service (DoS) if exploited. However, the attack is limited to local execution, reducing its overall risk.
recommended_actions: Update Assimp to a patched version once available or apply any recommended patches from the project maintainers.
Restrict local access to trusted users and ensure proper access controls are in place to mitigate exploitation.
Monitor for any unusual application crashes or behavior and apply additional logging to identify potential exploitation attempts.

title:
Sereal::Decoder versions before 5.005 for Perl allow heap out-of-bounds read via crafted input.
...
description:
Sereal::Decoder versions before 5.005 for Perl allow heap out-of-bounds read via crafted input.

In Perl/Decoder/srl_decoder.c, srl_read_object() and srl_read_hash() process a COPY tag, a back-reference whose target byte the decoder re-decodes as a fresh tag. When that target byte matches the SHORT_BINARY pattern (an inline string whose length is encoded in the low bits of the tag), the resulting read is not bounded to precede the COPY tag's own offset and can run past the end of the input buffe

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8796.

title: CVE-2026-8652 - An OS Command Injection vulnerability exists in Aterm. If a malicious third person gains administrat
description: An OS Command Injection vulnerability exists in Aterm. If a malicious third person gains administrator access to the product’s web console, they may be able to execute arbitrary OS commands via adjacent network.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8652.

title: CVE-2026-6059 - A cross-site scripting vulnerability exists in Aterm. Arbitrary scripts may be executed in the web b
description: A cross-site scripting vulnerability exists in Aterm. Arbitrary scripts may be executed in the web browser of a user accessing the web management interface via adjacent network.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in Aterm's web management interface allows an attacker on the adjacent network to execute arbitrary scripts in the victim's browser. This could lead to session hijacking, data theft, or unauthorized actions performed on behalf of the user.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest security patch or firmware update provided by the vendor to address CVE-2026-6059.
Restrict access to the web management interface to trusted IP addresses or use a VPN for secure access.
Educate users about the risks of accessing the management interface over untrusted networks and ensure strong authentication mechanisms are in place.

title: CVE-2026-9489 - NitroSense 3.x before 3.01.3052 contains Local Privilege Escalation (LPE) vulnerability.The program
description: NitroSense 3.x before 3.01.3052 contains Local Privilege Escalation (LPE) vulnerability.The program exposes a Windows Named Pipe that uses a custom protocol to invoke internal functions. However, this Named Pipe is misconfigured, allowing any authenticated local user to execute arbitrary code with NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM privileges and to delete arbitrary files with SYSTEM privileges. By leveraging this, an attacker can execute arbitrary code on the target system with elevated privileges.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated local users to escalate privileges to NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM, enabling them to execute arbitrary code and delete files with elevated permissions. This could lead to system compromise, data loss, or unauthorized access to sensitive information.
recommended_actions: Update NitroSense to version 3.01.3052 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Restrict access to the affected systems by limiting local user accounts and enforcing strong authentication mechanisms.
Monitor system logs for unusual activity, particularly attempts to access or exploit Windows Named Pipes.
